entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of business knowledge
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explain the basic aspects of insurance for small businesses (meaning, principles, importance, choice of appropriate insurance plan and procedures of filing insurance claims) Scenario: Share a short story to the students concerning different damages in a community. Then, guide students to relate the findings in the scenario by identifying: (i) Risks identified in the story; and (ii) Means of overcoming risks Relate their feedback to the meaning and importance of insurance Guest speaker: Invite a resource person, who is an expert on insurance, to come and share with students on the following: (i) principles of insurance; (ii) types of insurance; (iii) choice of appropriate insurance plan; (iv) procedures of obtaining insurance policy; and (v) procedures for filing insurance claims Let students write a report on what they learnt from the resource person in relation to principles of insurance, types of insurance and procedures for filing insurance claims Skills lab: Use manageable groups to discuss how they can obtain insurance for their business club and what strategies they can use to achieve this goal Project activity: Task students to show how they will apply their knowledge of insurance to obtain an insurance for their businesses and properties
entries.assessment: The basic aspects of insurance for small businesses are appropriately explained
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of business knowledge
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: (b) Describe business partnerships (meaning, types, features, advantages and disadvantages, formation and dissolution) Role play: Use manageable groups to role play features associated with running a business as a group Relate the role play to the meaning, features and formation of business partnership Case study: Provide a case study that portrays a partnership business. The case study should capture areas of formation and dissolution of a partnership. Let students discuss and share lessons learnt from the case study Debate: Guide the students to debate on this theme: “Running a business as a group is better than running it as an individual” Relate the students’ arguments in the debate to the advantages and disadvantages of partnership Skills lab: Guide students, in manageable groups, to develop a partnership deed using a partnership deed template provided to them. Let students present their draft partnership deed to receive feedback for further improvement Project activity: Guide the students to polish the partnership deed and use it in their business club. Monitor the progress from time to time
entries.assessment: Business partnership clearly described
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Solve social challenges using business skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Demonstrate mastery of the basic skills of operating a small-scale business
entries.learning_activity: (a) Describe taxes and levies associated with small businesses in Tanzania (meaning, types, importance, and sanctions for non-compliance) Role play: Direct students to act out a short play depicting a busy market in which tax/levy payers and collectors are in action. Then, ask the students to reflect on the meaning and importance of tax, and sanctions for non-compliance Guest speaker: Invite tax/levies officers from Local government and/or Tanzania Revenue Authority (TRA)/Zanzibar Revenue Authority (ZRA) to share their knowledge and experience on tax and levy issues Prepare guiding questions for the students based on the following: (i) Types of tax; and (ii) Tax assessment Summarise the lesson by linking the guest speaker’s experience to the types of tax and tax assessment Skills lab: Assign students to reflect on specific types of taxes/levies and tell how they can adopt (some of) them in their business club Let student present to the rest of class for feedback Project activity: Task students to write a report on the taxes and levies associated with small businesses in Tanzania
entries.assessment: Taxes and levies associated with small businesses in Tanzania are clearly described
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Solve social challenges using business skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Demonstrate mastery of the basic skills of operating a small-scale business
entries.learning_activity: (b) Describe the basic principles of communication and networking for small businesses Brainstorm activity: Guide students to think about two friends, how they became friends, and how they communicate Relate their responses to the meaning, importance, and channels of communication Scenario: Provide two short write ups such as a business letter or business plan (ideally one page), one with clear communication depicting all communication principles and another with unclear, confusing communication with grammatical errors Guide them to compare the two and identify which one is clearer and why. Relate the findings in the scenario to principles of communication Brainstorm Activity on networking: Guide students to share how they make new friends, why they make friends, and how helpful those friendships have been Relate their responses to the meaning and importance of business networking Skills lab: Task the students to create and write a plan on a loose sheet about how they will use principles of communication and business networking to grow their business project, which they will execute in their business clubs. Through the gallery walk, guide students to present the plans and receive feedback
entries.assessment: The basic principles of communication and networking for small businesses is clearly described
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Solve social challenges using business skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Demonstrate mastery of the basic skills of trading locally and internationally
entries.learning_activity: (a) Use pricing techniques to determine appropriate prices of products Scenario: Organise students in manageable groups and give each of them a scenario that portrays one type of pricing technique: (i) Cost-plus pricing; (ii) Value pricing; (iii) Penetration pricing; (iv) Price skimming; (v) Bundle pricing; (vi) Premium pricing; (vii) Competitive pricing; and (viii) Psychological pricing Guide students to summarise the lessons learnt from the scenario and relate the discussion with the meaning and importance of various pricing techniques. Field visit: Guide students to visit the nearest producer to investigate how demand, supply, cost, and competition influences the price determination of products Ask students to write a report based on a template provided by the teacher on what they have observed at the market and recommend on how they will price their business products based on the pricing techniques. Skills lab: Guide students, in manageable groups, to choose one product from their project and price it based on the techniques obtained from the scenario and/or field trip Let students present in order to receive feedback Project activity: Task students to create a price catalogue for their products based on the pricing techniques learnt
entries.assessment: Pricing techniques to determine appropriate prices of products are distinctly used
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Solve social challenges using business skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Demonstrate mastery of the basic skills of trading locally and internationally
entries.learning_activity: (b) Identify relevant procedures and practices for buying and selling products within and outside the country Scenario: Provide a short scenario of two young people whereby one wishes to sell a product to a neighbouring country and the other wishes to buy another product from that country but they are stranded because they do not have the know-how. Ask the students to advise the two young people Relate the advice from the students to the import and export procedures Case study: Organise students in manageable groups and provide two case studies that portray relevant exporting and importing procedures and practices. The cases should capture the areas of documentation, institutions, clearing and forwarding agencies, and incoterms. Let students discuss and share lessons learnt from the case study
entries.assessment: Relevant procedures and practices for buying and selling products within and outside the country are clearly identified
resources: sample documents for importation and exportation of products
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Solve social challenges using business skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Demonstrate mastery of the basic skills of trading locally and internationally
entries.learning_activity: (c) Use appropriate selling methods and techniques (branding, logos, packaging, after sales services, barcoding, direct mail and internet auctions) to facilitate trade Brainstorm: Guide students to share their experiences of buying and selling products or what they see happening in the community Relate the brainstorm results to the selling methods and techniques in preparation for the skills lab Skills lab: In groups let students choose one product from their club and then name the product, create/finetune the logo, design/improve packaging for the product and barcoding. Also, guide students to propose strategies of selling their products Then, guide students to present in class and provide feedback for further improvement of their proposal Project activity: Guide students in their manageable groups to go and improve products in their business club based on the lesson learnt from the skills lab
entries.assessment: Selling methods and techniques to facilitate trade is clearly demonstrated
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Apply business knowledge in various contexts
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Apply business theories into practice
entries.learning_activity: Complete and present the business plan developed in Form Three Group activity: Guide students to exchange their group business plan documents for peer review and further improvement. Students improve their business plans based on the peer feedback Provide feedback and allow them to complete their business plans in their skills lab Skills lab: Guide the students to polish and submit complete business plans. Task students in groups to prepare the pitch of their business plans, role play (rehearse) it, and pitch their business plans in the business club or to a panel of investors Project activity: Let the students prepare and present their portfolio folder comprising all useful documents covered in form four and submit them for assessment
entries.assessment: Business plan clearly presented
resources: business plans developed in Form III
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education
